panels.title: ISP Status
panels.description: Whether the Eero gateway has a working upstream internet connection.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_network_health" and r._field == "isp_up")
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _value: if r._value == true then 1 else 0}))

panels.title: Mesh Status
panels.description: Overall Eero mesh network health status.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_network_health" and r._field == "eero_network_status")
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _value: if r._value == "connected" then 1 else 0}))

panels.title: Double NAT
panels.description: Double NAT detected means your Eero is behind another router. This can cause issues with port forwarding and game hosting.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: -25h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_network_config" and r._field == "double_nat")
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _value: if r._value == true then 1 else 0}))

panels.title: Firmware Status
panels.description: Whether any Eero node has a firmware update available.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: -25h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_network_config" and r._field == "firmware_has_update")
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _value: if r._value == true then 1 else 0}))

panels.title: ISP Download Speed
panels.description: Last measured ISP download speed from Eero's built-in speed test (not real-time).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_isp_speed" and r._field == "speed_down_mbps")
  |> last()

panels.title: ISP Upload Speed
panels.description: Last measured ISP upload speed from Eero's built-in speed test (not real-time).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_isp_speed" and r._field == "speed_up_mbps")
  |> last()

panels.title: Network Configuration
panels.description: Live network config snapshot: GeoIP for WAN IP, DNS/DHCP setup, security features, and guest network status.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: -25h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_network_config")
  |> pivot(rowKey:["_time"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "geoip_city", "geoip_isp", "geoip_org", "geoip_region_name",
      "geoip_timezone", "public_ip", "dns_mode", "dhcp_mode", "wan_type",
      "band_steering", "wpa3_enabled", "upnp_enabled", "guest_network_name",
      "double_nat", "wireless_mode", "mlo_mode"])
  |> last(column: "_time")

panels.title: Connected Clients per Node
panels.description: Number of Wi-Fi clients currently associated to each Eero node. Useful for spotting imbalanced load across your mesh.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_node_timeseries" and r._field == "connected_clients_count")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.node_name =~ /^${Node:regex}$/)
  |> group(columns: ["node_name"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> yield(name: "mean")

panels.title: Node Uptime
panels.description: Per-node online/offline status over time. 'green' from the eero API means fully operational.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_node_timeseries" and r._field == "status")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.node_name =~ /^${Node:regex}$/)
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)
  |> yield(name: "last")

panels.title: Mesh Backhaul Quality
panels.description: Quality of wireless backhaul link between each node and the gateway (1–5 bars). 5=excellent, 1=critical. N/A for wired backhaul nodes.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: -15m)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_node_timeseries" and r._field == "mesh_quality_bars")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.node_name =~ /^${Node:regex}$/)
  |> last()

panels.title: Eero Hardware Details
panels.description: Static inventory: serial, model, IP, firmware version, and whether each node is the primary gateway.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: -2h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_node_metadata")
  |> pivot(rowKey:["serial"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> keep(columns: ["node_name", "serial", "model_number", "ip_address", "os_version",
      "wired", "is_primary_node", "update_available", "ethernet_addresses", "joined"])
  |> group()

panels.title: Firmware Status
panels.description: Current firmware on each node. 'update_available = true' rows indicate a pending update.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: -2h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_node_metadata")
  |> pivot(rowKey:["serial"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> keep(columns: ["node_name", "os_version", "update_available"])
  |> group()

panels.title: Clients on Node (History)
panels.description: Historical client count for the selected node over the chosen time range.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_node_timeseries" and r._field == "connected_clients_count")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.node_name =~ /^${Node:regex}$/)
  |> group(columns: ["node_name"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: Mesh Quality History
panels.description: Mesh backhaul quality bars (1–5) over time for the selected node. Drops may indicate interference or physical obstructions.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_node_timeseries" and r._field == "mesh_quality_bars")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.node_name =~ /^${Node:regex}$/)
  |> group(columns: ["node_name"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: Currently Connected Devices
panels.description: Devices currently associated to the selected node, with their MAC address and Wi-Fi band.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: -5m)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_client_timeseries" and r._field == "connected" and r._value == true)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.node_name =~ /^${Node:regex}$/)
  |> group(columns: ["device_name"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, device: r.device_name, mac: r.mac,
      connected_to: r.node_name,
      band: if r.frequency == "" or r.frequency == "wired" then "Wired" else "Frequency " + r.frequency + " " + r.frequency_unit
     }))
  |> group()

panels.title: Node Power & Backhaul
panels.description: Power source (USB, PoE) and backhaul connection type (wired/wireless) for the selected node over time.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: -5m)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_node_timeseries" and
      (r._field == "power_source" or r._field == "connection_type"))
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.node_name =~ /^${Node:regex}$/)
  |> last()

panels.title: Band Distribution
panels.description: Breakdown of currently connected clients by Wi-Fi band. Wired = ethernet-connected clients.

panels.title: Band Steering Efficiency
panels.description: Number of connected devices on each frequency band over time. Shows how well devices are distributed across 2.4/5/6 GHz.

targets.query:
import "date"
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_client_timeseries" and r._field == "connected" and r._value == true)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.frequency != "")
  |> group(columns: ["frequency", "frequency_unit"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: count, createEmpty: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({
       r with frequency:
         if r.frequency == "wired" then "Wired"
         else "Frequency " + r.frequency + " " + r.frequency_unit
     }))

panels.title: Peak Hours (7-day avg)
panels.description: Average number of devices connected per hour of day over the last 7 days. Useful for spotting peak network usage patterns.

targets.query:
import "date"
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: -7d)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_client_timeseries" and r._field == "connected" and r._value == true)
  |> group(columns: ["_time"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: count, createEmpty: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with hour: string(v: date.hour(t: r._time))}))
  |> group(columns: ["hour"])
  |> mean(column: "_value")

panels.title: Network-Wide Signal Strength
panels.description: RSSI (dBm) for every wireless client. Closer to 0 is better; below -75 dBm is poor.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_client_timeseries" and r._field == "signal")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.connection_type == "wireless")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.node_name =~ /^${Node:regex}$/)
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: Global Throughput Rates ($Frequency)
panels.description: ⚠️ Negotiated Wi-Fi link (PHY) rates — NOT real-time throughput. Shows the max speed the radio association is capable of. A device shows a high rate even when idle.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_client_timeseries" and
      (r._field == "rx_rate_bps" or r._field == "tx_rate_bps"))
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.frequency =~ /^${Frequency:regex}$/)
  |> group(columns: ["device_name"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> yield(name: "mean")

panels.title: AP Roaming Events
panels.description: Which Eero node the selected device is associated with over time. Changes indicate a roaming event.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_client_timeseries" and r._field == "signal")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device_name =~ /^${Device:regex}$/)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "device_name", "node_name"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, _value: r.node_name, device_name: r.device_name}))

panels.title: Device Signal Strength
panels.description: RSSI (dBm) for the selected device over time. Closer to 0 is better; below -75 dBm is poor.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_client_timeseries" and r._field == "signal")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device_name =~ /^${Device:regex}$/)
  |> group(columns: ["device_name"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> yield(name: "mean")

panels.title: Device Metadata
panels.description: Current connection snapshot for the selected device: connected node, MAC, frequency band, and connection type.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_client_timeseries" and r._field == "connected")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device_name =~ /^${Device:regex}$/)
  |> group(columns: ["device_name"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({
       _time: r._time,
       device: r.device_name,
       mac: r.mac,
       connected_to: r.node_name,
       connection: r.connection_type,
       band: if r.frequency == "" or r.frequency == "wired" then "Wired"
             else "Frequency " + r.frequency + " " + r.frequency_unit
     }))
  |> group()

panels.title: Offline / Stale Devices (>24h)
panels.description: Devices that were last seen connected more than 24 hours ago. Useful for tracking unplugged or forgotten devices.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: -25h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_client_timeseries" and r._field == "connected")
  |> group(columns: ["device_name"])
  |> last()
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._value == false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, device: r.device_name, mac: r.mac,
      last_seen: string(v: r._time), connected_to: r.node_name}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Paused Devices
panels.description: Devices currently paused by an Eero profile (parental controls or manual pause).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: -25h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_client_timeseries" and r._field == "paused")
  |> group(columns: ["device_name"])
  |> last()
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._value == true)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, device: r.device_name, mac: r.mac}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Blocked Devices
panels.description: Devices that have been blocked/blacklisted from the network.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "eero")
  |> range(start: -25h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eero_client_timeseries" and r._field == "blacklisted")
  |> group(columns: ["device_name"])
  |> last()
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._value == true)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, device: r.device_name, mac: r.mac}))
  |> group()
